title: Algonquin Legends of New England
description: Algonquin Legends of New England — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: Abistanooch
memes.description: The Marten — a companion of Glooskap and a trickster figure in his own right, known for his cleverness in capturing a fairy wife and his contentious friendship with Team the Moose.

memes.label: Annapolis Valley
memes.description: The Annapolis Valley of Nova Scotia appears in Algonquin legends as the setting for a Glooskap story in which the culture hero transformed the landscape by fording the valley and interacting with Beaver.

memes.label: The Great Ball Game of Glooskap
memes.description: Two legendary ball games in the Glooskap cycle — one in which Mikchich the Turtle evades his enemies by leaping over the lodge, and another in which Glooskap confronts evil magicians on the sandy plain of Saco River, playing with a living skull that snaps at his heels.

memes.label: Bay of Fundy
memes.description: The great tidal bay between New Brunswick and Nova Scotia, whose dramatic tides and dense fogs provide the setting for several Wabanaki legends collected by Charles Godfrey Leland.

memes.label: Cape Blomidon
memes.description: Cape Blomidon, a prominent headland on the Bay of Fundy in Nova Scotia, is one of the most significant landmarks in Glooskap legends—said to be the site where Glooskap built a great dam and where the agates found on its shores carry supernatural tradition.

memes.label: Bumole
memes.description: The Spirit of Night (also called Pamola) in Passamaquoddy mythology — a powerful supernatural being associated with the night air, darkness, and the spirit world.

memes.label: Campobello
memes.description: Campobello Island, New Brunswick, is where Charles G. Leland collected Wabanaki legends among the Passamaquoddy Indians beginning in the summer of 1882, making it the primary fieldwork site for the documentation of Northeastern Algonquin mythology.

memes.label: How Glooskap Sailed Through the Great Cavern of Darkness
memes.description: A Micmac tradition recounting how Glooskap sailed through the great Cavern of Darkness — an underground river journey of death and rebirth that marked his attainment of the greatest power and carries deep resonances with Wabeno mystery initiation.

memes.label: Chenoo
memes.description: A terrifying cannibal ice-giant of Wabanaki mythology — a human being transformed by cannibalism into a freezing, stone-skinned monster who can be redeemed through human kindness and the purging of ice from its body.

memes.label: How Glooskap Made the Elves and Fairies
memes.description: The Passamaquoddy creation account of how Glooskap, coming first into the Wabanaki land, made the Mikumwess (forest elves), the water fairies (Oonahgemessuk), and then Man from the bark of Ash-trees — the foundational cosmogonic event of the Algonquin mythic world.

memes.label: Fredericton
memes.description: Fredericton, the capital city of New Brunswick, was home to Edward Jack, a key informant who contributed several Mi'kmaq legends to Charles Godfrey Leland's collection, including the tale of Tumilkoontaoo (the Broken Wing).

memes.label: Gaspé Peninsula
memes.description: The Gaspé Peninsula, with its Shick-shoe Mountains, appears in Algonquin legends as a landscape shaped by Glooskap and associated with the Gaspereanx, a legendary figure or feature of the region.

memes.label: How Glooskap Fought the Giant Sorcerers at Saco
memes.description: A Penobscot tradition recounting how Glooskap confronted and transformed a family of giant sorcerers at Saco into fish — a defining act of divine justice against those who oppressed and devoured humankind.

memes.label: Glooskap
memes.description: Central culture hero and divinity of the Wabanaki (Northeastern Algonquin) mythology, who freed the world from monsters and departed with a promise to return.

memes.label: How Glooskap is Making Arrows for a Great Battle
memes.description: The Passamaquoddy eschatological tradition of how Glooskap endlessly fashions arrows in a distant wigwam, preparing for a final apocalyptic battle that will destroy the world.
aliases: The Twilight of the Indian Gods

memes.label: The Birth of Glooskap and the Death of Malsum
memes.description: The foundational Wabanaki origin event in which the culture hero Glooskap and his evil twin brother Malsum the Wolf are born, Malsum kills their mother, and Glooskap ultimately destroys Malsum using the one weapon that can harm him — a fern root.

memes.label: Glooskap's Departure
memes.description: Glooskap's departure from the world — marked by a great feast by Lake Minas where all beasts gathered before dispersing — ends the mythic age with a promise to return and restore the Golden Age.

memes.label: How Glooskap Went to England and France
memes.description: The Passamaquoddy tradition of how Glooskap sailed his stone canoe across the Atlantic, discovered England and France before Europeans knew of America, and had his adopted mother baptized — a tale blending ancient myth with post-contact adaptation.

memes.label: How Glooskap Found the Summer
memes.description: The Micmac tradition of how Glooskap journeyed to the land of the Queen of Summer, brought Summer back in his bosom, and established the cycle of seasons in the Wabanaki world — one of the most beloved seasonal myths in Algonquin legend.

memes.label: The Great Dance of the Mikumwess
memes.description: At the wedding feast following a young man's trials, the Mikumwess performed a great dance in which his feet sank ever deeper into the hard-beaten floor, ploughing the ground into high ridges until only his head was visible — a display of magical power that marked the end of dancing for ordinary mortals that night.

memes.label: Keanke
memes.description: A figure who appears alongside Glooskap in the illustration of the whale-spearing episode — mentioned in the narrative of Glooskap's great frolic with Kitpooseagunow.

memes.label: Keekwahjoo
memes.description: Keekwahjoo (the Badger) is a figure in Mi'kmaq oral tradition who appears as the elder pilgrim in a Wabanaki migration narrative and as a rogue in the Lox trickster cycle

memes.label: Keeoony
memes.description: Keeoony (the Otter) is a figure in Wabanaki oral tradition who features in a rivalry tale with Master Rabbit, in which the Otter's superior lodge-building and fishing skills provoke Rabbit's envy

memes.label: Kitpooseagunow
memes.description: A mighty magician-giant in Algonquin mythology, second only to Glooskap — born after his mother's death and renowned as the Protector of the Oppressed, who shared a great frolic of whale-catching and frost-making with the culture hero.

memes.label: Kookwess
memes.description: A cannibal giant and sorcerer in Passamaquoddy mythology, whose terrible roar could kill all who heard it — one of the monstrous adversaries overcome by Glooskap.

memes.label: Labrador
memes.description: The vast northeastern peninsula of North America, whose subarctic coastline marks the northern limit of the Wabanaki cultural world and the meeting point of Algonquian and Eskimo traditions as documented in Algonquin Legends of New England.

memes.label: Lox
memes.description: The great trickster and malevolent magician of Wabanaki mythology, appearing variously as a wolverine, wolf, lynx, or raccoon across the Algonquin legends collected by Charles Godfrey Leland.

memes.label: Lusifee
memes.description: The Loup-Cervier (Wild Cat / Canada Lynx) — the obstinate natural predator of Master Rabbit in Micmac mythology, whose relentless pursuit of his prey ends in humiliation through the Rabbit's magical trickery.

memes.label: Malsum
memes.description: Malsum the Wolf is the evil twin brother of Glooskap in Wabanaki creation mythology, representing the opposing force of evil and destruction against Glooskap's creative benevolence.

memes.label: The Adventures of Master Rabbit
memes.description: A Wabanaki trickster tale cycle featuring Master Rabbit (Mahtigwess), whose vain attempts to rival other beings and gain social status invariably end in humiliation — one of the most fully preserved trickster cycles in Algonquin Legends of New England.

memes.label: Mikchich
memes.description: The Turtle, Glooskap's uncle, whom the culture hero transformed from a poor old man into a great and handsome figure — a foundational story of the Turtle totem among the Wabanaki.

memes.label: How Glooskap Made His Uncle Mikchich the Turtle into a Great Man
memes.description: The Micmac and Passamaquoddy legend of how Glooskap transformed his poor, lazy, and aging uncle Mikchich into a handsome young man, won him a wife, and ultimately made him the great chief of the Tortoises — explaining the origin of the Turtle totem, among the highest-ranking among the Algonquins.

memes.label: Mi'kmaq
memes.description: An Algonquian-speaking Indigenous people of the Wabanaki Confederacy, whose oral traditions constitute one of the three principal tribal sources for the legends collected in Algonquin Legends of New England.

memes.label: Mikumwess
memes.description: Mikumwess is the Indian Puck or Robin Good-Fellow of Wabanaki folklore — a forest elf or fairy figure who wears a red cap like the Norse Goblin and represents the minor mythology of dwarfs, fairies, and nature spirits.

memes.label: Mooin
memes.description: Mooin (the Bear) appears in Algonquin legends as a prominent animal character with his own chapter in the Glooskap cycle, "Of the Adventure with Mooin, the Bear"

memes.label: M'teoulin
memes.description: The M'teoulin is the Wabanaki magician or shaman, whose doctrine taught that every created thing possessed an indwelling spirit, forming a system of animistic belief with striking parallels to European esoteric traditions.

memes.label: New Brunswick
memes.description: The province of New Brunswick, Canada—traditional homeland of the Maliseet, Mi'kmaq, and Passamaquoddy peoples—is the primary geographic setting for many of the Algonquin legends collected by Charles Godfrey Leland in the 1880s.

memes.label: Nova Scotia
memes.description: Nova Scotia (Mi'kma'ki), a Maritime province of Canada and the traditional homeland of the Mi'kmaq people, is the geographic setting for many of the Algonquin legends collected by Charles Godfrey Leland and the Rev. Silas T. Rand.

memes.label: Oochigeaskw
memes.description: Oochigeaskw (the Burnt-Faced Girl) is the heroine of a Micmac Cinderella-like tale, "The Invisible One," in which an abused youngest daughter sees what her proud sisters cannot and wins the hand of a celestial being

memes.label: Oscoon
memes.description: A leader of the people in Passamaquoddy mythology — called "the Liver" — who saved his community from the cannibal giant Kookwess through wisdom and courage, and was afterward made chief.

memes.label: Partridge Island
memes.description: Partridge Island (Munegoo in Mi'kmaq), an island at the mouth of the Bay of Fundy, appears in Algonquin legends as a landmark associated with Glooskap's travels and encounters with Kitpooseagunow, the spirit of the partridge.

memes.label: Passamaquoddy
memes.description: An Algonquian-speaking Indigenous people of the Wabanaki Confederacy, whose oral traditions form a significant portion of the legends collected in Algonquin Legends of New England.

memes.label: Penobscot
memes.description: An Algonquian-speaking Indigenous people of Maine and the Wabanaki Confederacy, whose oral traditions form a key source for the legends collected in Algonquin Legends of New England.

memes.label: Piktook
memes.description: Piktook (present-day Pictou, Nova Scotia) is a Mi'kmaq settlement that appears in a Glooskap legend where the culture hero visits a feast, encounters the chief's daughter, and competes for her hand against the young men of the village.

memes.label: Glooskap and Pook-jin-skwess the Evil Pitcher
memes.description: The Passamaquoddy tale of Glooskap's encounter with Pook-jin-skwess, the Cannibal Witch — a monster whose round feet, pointed heels, and appetite for human flesh mark her as one of the most fearsome adversaries Glooskap defeated, and whose transformation into a mosquito explains the origin of that tormenting insect.

memes.label: Pulowech
memes.description: The Partridge — a Micmac hero and sorcerer who appears as a type of Glooskap in Algonquin mythology, known for his adventures with sea-maidens, cannibal sorcerers, and magical contests.

memes.label: Sebayk
memes.description: Sebayk, a Passamaquoddy settlement near Campobello Island at the mouth of the Bay of Fundy, was known in Algonquin legends as a community where the tradition of m'teoulin (shamanic magic) remained active into the late 19th century.

memes.label: St. John River
memes.description: The St. John River (Wolastoq), a major waterway in present-day New Brunswick and the traditional heartland of the Maliseet (Wolastoqiyik) people, featured in several Algonquin legends as a setting for magical encounters and hunting expeditions.

memes.label: Team
memes.description: Team (the Moose) is a figure in Passamaquoddy oral tradition who lived with Abistanooch the Marten and features in a tale of hospitality, betrayal, and supernatural retaliation

memes.label: Tomah Josephs
memes.description: A renowned Passamaquoddy storyteller, Indian Governor, and primary informant for Charles Godfrey Leland's collection of Algonquin legends — the source of many of the most important narratives in the corpus.

memes.label: Wabanaki
memes.description: The Wabanaki ("Children of Light" or "People of the Dawn") are the Northeastern Algonquin peoples — primarily the Micmac, Passamaquoddy, Penobscot, and St. Francis tribes — who share a grand mythology centered on the culture hero Glooskap.

memes.label: How Glooskap Strode with the Mighty Wasis
memes.description: The Passamaquoddy tale of how Glooskap, the all-powerful Master of Men and Beasts, was shamefully defeated by the Mighty Wasis — a baby — in one of the most beloved and instructive episodes of the Glooskap cycle, teaching that even the greatest power must yield to the force of sheer infant will.

memes.label: Wejek
memes.description: The Tree Partridge — companion of Pulowech in Micmac mythology, who rashly pursued the cannibal sorcerers who devoured his friend's wife and was himself slain.

memes.label: Win-pe
memes.description: Win-pe is a powerful sorcerer in Wabanaki mythology who stole Glooskap's family, triggering a pursuit and confrontation that forms one of the central narratives of the Glooskap cycle.

memes.label: How Glooskap Bound Wuchowsen, the Great Wind-Bird
memes.description: The Passamaquoddy tradition of how Glooskap bound Wuchowsen, the great Wind-Bird whose flapping wings caused ceaseless gales — and how his restraint brought stillness to the world until Glooskap wisely restored a measured wind, establishing balance in the elements.
